title: The battle for LB9.0↓
content: I'm amazed by the incredible results being submitted one after another. How did these admirable competitors manage to break through this barrier?

comments.content:
My first question is always: do I understand the question? Do I understand the data?

From there: how would I (as a human) learn to make this prediction? then, how can I combine this with code? Is it logic that I need, maths, patterns, etc…

Do you understand the data and have you played with it enough? I know there is a lot in this data that took me AGES to wrap my head around. Talk to a LLM about some EDA, explore your ideas, nothing is wrong if you learn even one thing from it.

replies.content:
Thank you for your encouraging words!!

I'm still inexperienced, so I'll review my EDA (Electronic Data Analysis) process.

3.5… that's a very frightening score.

By the way, is there any basis for that predicted score?
that's oracle score. You can do mock test, it would give you similar score.
That's quite a lot better than we currently have… It is not linear effort to reduce a RMSE score linearly…
Who knows. Maybe EDA god @cdeotte will release a secret to reach 3.5 next week and tell you that's logarithm effort.

comments.content: We created some features, trained models, blended some approaches. And like 80-90% of my ideas don't work. It is important to not give up and pursue different ideas.

replies.content: do you think there is a data leakage ? or people just push the boundary really well ?
